# Vibe Coding Portfolio | AI Judgment Evidence by Lucas Sung

Canonical URL: https://lucasung-debug.github.io/hermes-ops-dashboard-page/

This site is Lucas Sung's public evidence surface for HR DX automation cases described in his career portfolio. It is not the written career/job portfolio. Its purpose is to show the user's durable AI capability: narrowing the question, verifying AI output, and deciding what to keep or discard.

## Flagship evidence cases

- Condolence flower order automation: HR operations workflow for standardizing condolence flower requests that were previously handled through KakaoTalk or text. Evidence flow: question the requester/owner/vendor/accounting split, verify login-to-sheet behavior with invalid inputs, keep the standard form and automatic notifications. Metric: 0 human errors, about 90% less repetitive administration. Demo: demos/gyeongjo-flow.mp4. Source: https://github.com/lucasung-debug/gyeongjo-flow.
- E-signature collection program: Mobile web flow for collecting pledges from 550 production workers without personal PCs. Evidence flow: accept the no-PC field constraint, verify finger signature input and image integrity, discard the PC process and unify on mobile. Metric: all 550 signatures collected, a two-week task became three days. Demo: demos/hr-esign.mp4. Source: https://github.com/lucasung-debug/hr-documents. Live: https://hr-documents.vercel.app.
- Job keyword analysis agent: AI-assisted text mining workflow for converting interviews and surveys into role-specific competencies. Evidence flow: ask whether fixing AI as a job analyst can reduce interpretation variance, verify stable output for the same input, keep one classification standard. Metric: quantified qualitative data and expanded to 600 external applicant EVP analyses. Demo: demos/job-keyword-mining.mp4. Source: https://github.com/lucasung-debug/job-keyword-mining.
- Recruit poster generator: Public recruiting-promotion tool that converts job data into branded poster images. Evidence flow: identify brand-tone drift, verify data structuring, copy generation, and result review in a public tool, choose generation tooling over manual design. Metric: posters come directly from sheet data or AI-made data. Demo: demos/ottogi-recruit.mp4. Source: https://github.com/lucasung-debug/ottogi-recruit. Live: https://ottogi-recruit.vercel.app.

## Learning archive

- HTMLvault: redirected and waiting. Markdown was judged better than HTML for knowledge management; the project can return when enough knowledge accumulates.
- mnemo-hook: replaced. It connected session context directly, but Obsidian and hermes automated records became better.
- hr-newsletter-automation: paused with a planned restart. It was an actually operated HR tool, paused after leaving the HR role.
- moneyfit: converting to app. The web tool is being converted into an app with an AI agent.

## AEO/GEO structure

The page should remain easy for AI answer engines to quote:

- Keep a clear title and meta description.
- Keep canonical URL metadata.
- Keep Open Graph and Twitter metadata.
- Keep JSON-LD WebSite, Person, and ItemList data aligned with the 4 evidence cases.
- Keep the on-screen narrative aligned to questioning, verification, and judgment.
- Prefer demo videos, GitHub repository URLs, live URLs, README/spec evidence, and career portfolio anchors as evidence.

## Exposure rules

- Avoid secrets and private implementation details.
- Do not present learning archive items as verified flagship DX evidence.
- Keep the career portfolio as context and this site as evidence.
